meta.title: "How are you?" 말고 — 회의 첫 30초 small talk

pda.persona: 해외 팀/클라이언트와 정기 미팅하는 직장인 — 본론은 되는데 오프닝 30초에서 늘 어색함
pda.desire: 원어민 동료와 자연스럽게 대화 여는 감각

data.title:
"How are you?" 말고
이렇게 열어보세요
data.bg_prompt: Wide editorial documentary photograph of a busy modern corporate meeting room high in a Manhattan skyscraper, foreground group of three to four multicultural professionals including a Korean woman and her Western colleagues standing together near floor-to-ceiling windows greeting each other with handshakes, warm genuine smiles, and open gestures, these foreground subjects captured tack sharp in crisp focus, in the background several other office workers walking past in the corridor and the far side of the room rendered with natural motion blur as if caught on a slightly slow shutter, a sense of a real working day in motion around the central greeting, strong afternoon backlight through the windows with a vivid clear blue New York sky and crisp detailed Manhattan skyline of tall office buildings behind them, subjects rim-lit and softly silhouetted while balanced exposure keeps their faces readable, wearing contemporary tailored business attire in neutral tones, long wooden conference table and leather chairs in the foreground with small lived-in details like coffee cups an open folder and pens on the surface, natural warm color palette with gentle contrast, candid reportage feel in the style of a Harvard Business Review or New York Times business section photo essay, wide full-body composition with the camera pulled back to include the ceiling and the full height of the windows, subjects grouped center-right leaving generous negative space on the left for the skyline to breathe, authentic unposed on-location moment, high resolution, sharp modern look with selective motion blur only on background activity, no text, no logo, no laptop, no monitor, no phone

data.header: 같은 질문, 같은 대답, 같은 어색함
data.body:
"How are you?"
→ "Good. And you?"
→ "Good."

여기서 말이 끊기고
바로 본론으로 들어가죠.

원어민들은 이 30초에
다른 걸 하고 있었어요.

data.headline:
How’s your week 
going so far?
data.bg_prompt: Two professionals standing face-to-face near floor-to-ceiling windows with the Manhattan skyline behind them, a Korean professional smiling mid-small-talk while a Western colleague nods attentively listening, 3/4 rear-side angle showing their body language during a brief greeting moment, wood paneled meeting room with leather chairs softly blurred in the background, diffused daylight mixed with overhead practical fluorescent, flat lighting with low contrast, muted saturated color palette of mustard yellow warm gray brown and olive, 90s American TV drama aesthetic reminiscent of Ally McBeal and early Suits, 16mm film grain texture, subtle VHS color shift, no laptop, no screen, no monitor

items.label: icon_x
items.eng: How are you? / Good, and you? / Good.
items.kor: 의례적 인사로 끝. 서로 어색해지는 공백이 생김

items.label: icon_check
items.eng: How's your week going so far?
items.kor: 구체적 질문 → 상대가 실제로 대답할 수 있는 오프닝

items.title: How's your week going so far?
items.body:
이번 주 어떻게 지내고 계세요?
평일 회의에 가장 무난

items.title: How was your weekend?
items.body:
주말 어떠셨어요?
월요일 오전 회의에 자연스러움

items.title: Anything exciting this weekend?
items.body:
이번 주말에 뭐 재밌는 일 있으세요?
금요일 오후 회의에 적합

items.title: Oh, that sounds great!
items.body:
좋은 이야기에 공감
휴가/주말 활동 답변에

items.title: That's a lot. Hope it calms down soon.
items.body:
바쁘다는 답변에 공감
톤을 맞춰 가볍게 위로

data.body:
영어권 비즈니스 문화에서
회의 전 small talk은 본론만큼이나
신뢰를 쌓는 시간이에요.

매번 "Good."으로만 끝나면
상대는 내가 대화에 관심이 없다고 느껴요.

30초만 바꿔도
남은 29분 30초의 온도가 달라집니다.

월요일 아침
→ How was your weekend?

평일 낮
→ How's your week going so far?

금요일 오후
→ Anything exciting this weekend?

caption:
💼 영어 회의 시작 30초, 늘 어색하지 않으세요?

"How are you?" — "Good. And you?" — "Good."
여기서 말이 끊기고, 바로 본론으로 들어가게 되죠.

원어민들은 이 30초에 다른 걸 하고 있었어요.

❝ 회의 오프닝 small talk.zip ❞
‣ How's your week going so far? — 이번 주 어떻게 지내세요?
‣ How was your weekend? — 주말 어떠셨어요?
‣ Anything exciting this weekend? — 이번 주말에 뭐 재밌는 일 있으세요?
‣ Oh, that sounds great! — 좋은 이야기에 공감
‣ Same here, honestly. — 저도 그래요

저장하기 꾹 누르고, 다음 회의 전에 하나씩 꺼내보세요 ✨

📩 더 많은 비즈니스 영어 표현, 꾸준히 받아보세요!
